completed_tasks.name: Fixed youtube_ingest.py max_n config key mismatch (was reading max_per_run, config uses max_n — per-channel limits silently ignored)

Root-caused + fixed yt-dlp over-fetch: bare channel-root URLs expand to up to 3 tab playlists (Videos/Shorts/Live) and -I 1:N applies PER TAB (NATO: 15 for max_n=5). Fixed by pinning URLs to /videos via _videos_tab_url()
Surfaced yt-dlp non-zero exits to stderr (were silently parsed as 'empty channel')
Added 2 regression tests (max_n reaches yt_dlp_list — verified fails on pre-fix code via stash; _videos_tab_url pinning). Suite: 7/7 pass
Rebuilt + redeployed ingest-youtube container, live-verified: NATO returns exactly 5 for max_n=5

Updated .env YT_CHANNELS per user: @bellingcat → @BellingcatOfficial (verified real channel), dropped @theisw (contentless via yt-dlp API) and @NRKnyheter (404). Container recreated, fresh run: zero errors, every feed exactly max_n (Bellingcat 10, NATO 5, Karpathy 5, NVIDIA 5). Postgres: 45 yt articles

remaining_tasks.name: Fix CR-01 (05-REVIEW.md): RabbitMQBus._rebuild_topology() deletes all 5 production queues on any AMQP 406, no dev/prod gate
Give triage worker's PostgresStore reconnect-on-SQL-error logic (mirrors RabbitMQ connect_robust) — poisoned-connection incident recurs otherwise
Architectural: point DEV_DSN at a genuinely separate test DB (shared dev/test Postgres footgun)
Decide cleanup of stale feed XMLs for dropped channels (youtube-isw.xml, youtube-nrk-nyheter.xml, youtube-youtubeofficial.xml) + corresponding FreshRSS subscriptions
Delete .env.bak-channels once user confirms channel list is right
Phase 6 (Brief app) — not started, no CONTEXT.md/PLAN.md. /gsd-discuss-phase 6 is the entry point

blockers.description: CR-01: RabbitMQBus._rebuild_topology() deletes all 5 production queues (not just the mismatched one) on any AMQP 406, no dev/prod gate. A routine redeploy changing any queue argument could silently drop every in-flight message.
blockers.workaround: None applied — advisory per code-review gate design, does not block Phase 6.

blockers.description: Shared dev/test Postgres: DEV_DSN (test fixtures) IS the live Postgres the docker-compose stack uses. Marker bugs fixed (7fd0023) but the architecture is unchanged.
blockers.workaround: Before running any Postgres-touching test file, verify its postgres-param variants carry the real pytest.mark.db_live marker; prefer -m 'not db_live'.

human_actions_pending.action: Confirm final YT_CHANNELS list — @theisw and @NRKnyheter were BOTH dropped on best judgment (user message was mangled by a path autocomplete; AskUserQuestion timed out). Re-add either from .env.bak-channels if only one was meant
human_actions_pending.context: Both handles were broken (404 / contentless), so dropping both was safe, but the user's exact intent was never confirmed.

human_actions_pending.action: Decide whether ISW and NRK Nyheter need replacement sources (no working YouTube handle found for either; @NRK is general NRK, not Nyheter)
human_actions_pending.context: ISW and NRK news coverage is now absent from youtube ingest.
Phase 5 fully closed; Phase 6 has no CONTEXT.md yet.

decisions.decision: Pinned yt-dlp channel URLs to the /videos tab (skip Shorts/Live) rather than truncating the merged list to max_n
decisions.rationale: Root cause was per-tab application of -I 1:N; /videos gives deterministic, shorts-free results appropriate for triage. URLs already naming a tab are left untouched.

decisions.decision: Dropped BOTH @theisw and @NRKnyheter when the user's 'drop @…' message was mangled and the clarifying question timed out
decisions.rationale: Both were verifiably broken (NRKnyheter 404s; theisw lists no tabs/videos via yt-dlp), so keeping either only produced silent-empty ingests. Old line preserved in .env.bak-channels for trivial rollback.
Not deployed in docker-compose, internally consistent (its own docstring documents max_per_run). Surgical-changes rule: not part of the request.

decisions.decision: Did not edit FreshRSS subscriptions or delete stale feed XMLs for dropped channels
decisions.rationale: Separate user-facing decision; removing the XMLs would 404 existing FreshRSS subscriptions.

next_action: Either start Phase 6 (/gsd-discuss-phase 6 — no CONTEXT.md yet) or pick up one of the two carried advisories (CR-01 topology-rebuild fix, PostgresStore reconnect-on-error). Confirm the YT_CHANNELS list with the user first if they're present.
context_notes: Short operational session between phases: resumed from prior pause, knocked out both pending youtube-ingest bugs (max_n key + per-tab over-fetch, one commit 0c6d75e, deployed and live-verified with exact counts), then updated the channel list per a partially-mangled user request. Phase 5 remains fully closed; Phase 6 untouched. Full docker-compose stack up and healthy — nothing needs restarting on resume. 45 yt articles in Postgres, DLQ empty.
